title: Does OS Security Policy profiles affect Podman
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT? **

/kind bug
<!---
/kind feature
-->
**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
Unable to use podman to create container. 

```
rootuser ~#] podman run -p 9200:9200 -p 9300:9300 -e "discovery.type=single-node" --name elasticsearch docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch-oss:7.9.2-amd64
```
**Output error:**

```
Error: /usr/bin/runc: error while loading shared libraries: libpthread.so.0: cannot open shared object file: Operation not permitted: OCI runtime permission denied error
```
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install RHEL 8.3 minimal and Container Management environment with Security Policy enabled and DISA STIG profile/content.

2. 
```
rootuser ~#] fips-mode-setup --check
FIPS mode is enabled.

rootuser ~#] update-crypto-policies --show
FIPS:OSPP
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
I was expected podman to create the container successfully. 

``podman run`` had no issues creating a container with the same security policy profile in RHEL 8.2 . However, the latest podman version in RHEL 8.2 is 1.9.x which doesn't provide support for IPv6.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

1. The error is consistent if the system was provisioning with security profile is enabled. Log file attached

2. Podman works fine with if the system was built without a security profile.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      2.0.0-rc7
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.18-1.module+el8.3.0+7084+c16098dd.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.18, commit: 7fd3f71a218f8d3a7202e464252aeb1e942d17eb'
  cpus: 1
  distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.3"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: rhelpod.vnet1.net
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 4.18.0-221.el8.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 995037184
  memTotal: 1905205248
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-66.rc10.module+el8.3.0+7084+c16098dd.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 2210394112
  swapTotal: 2210394112
  uptime: 4h 49m 4.84s (Approximately 0.17 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.redhat.io
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 1
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 1
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 1
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 1
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
  GitCommit: ""

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.0-0.9.rc7.module+el8.3.0+7084+c16098dd.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No, podman-2.0.0-0.9.rc7 is the latest version in RHEL 8.3

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Both Virtualbox 6.1 and VMWare vSphere 6.5
[debug.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5412215/debug.log)

comments.body: I recommend you file a Bugzilla about this one - Podman should definitely be working with FIPS mode enabled.

Would be interesting to see if this works with crun.  But runc was tested with fips mode.

Could you see if this works with a --privileged container?
If yes, then check to see if SELinux is causing issues.
@rhatdan, adding --privileged=true results with the same error message. I'll check Bugzilla. Thanks

@rhatdan, I'm waiting for the RHEL 8.3.1 release. You commented on my [bugzilla](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1890707) request that the latest RHEL release will have crun.  And since I enable CGroups v2, the default OCI runtime for podman changes to ``crun``.  

```
rootuser ~#] podman version
Error: default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument
```

The error with CGroups v1 was:
```
Error: /usr/bin/runc: error while loading shared libraries: libpthread.so.0: cannot open shared object file: Operation not permitted: OCI runtime permission denied error
```
Ok let's handle this in the bugzilla, this is not an upstream issue.
